digest.component_or_area: op-cert verification CI lane (mirror of verify-identity merge-gate)
propositions: Add a required-environment op-cert verification lane that reuses the verify-identity merged-log walk and grandfathering rather than a parallel walker.

A new CLI entrypoint rebar verify-opcert reads verify.require_environment and verify.opcert_enforce_since from config, groups the merged log by ticket, and verifies each in-scope ticket's completion-verifier op-cert, exiting 1 only when an enforced ticket lacks a valid required-environment op-cert.

The enforcement anchor for a ticket lacking an op-cert is the commit introducing its terminal close STATUS event, resolved and tested against verify.opcert_enforce_since so pre-boundary closures are grandfathered.
The lane stays advisory until enforce-since covers op-cert-bearing commits and require-environment is set, so it does not flip the repo to blocking before producers exist.

Security finding #4: the gate verifies the cert against material_fingerprint/merged_log_commit read from the same attacker-writable stored record, a circular check that lets a self-consistent stale cert pass.

Fix per verify-side authoritative-input rule: recompute material_fingerprint from authoritative ticket state and take merged_log_commit only from the signature-verified payload, constrained to be an ancestor of main and to satisfy era/enforce-since floors, failing closed otherwise.
